Zone Overview

Every zone is planned or locked. This document covers the new plant additions for 2026.

Canna 'Tropicanna Black' in aqua ceramic
Backyard · Slab Edge
Canna 'Tropicanna' in largest aqua ceramic
Front Door

Bog Garden

Backyard · Statement Containers

The Aqua Ceramic Trio

Three modern aqua ceramics anchor the yard's visual triangle — drawing the eye inward and away from the fence line. Each planted as a bold, single-species statement.

Ceramic A · Gazebo (Large Pot)
Canna 'Tropicanna Black'
Canna × generalis
Deep near-black burgundy foliage — bold, tropical, architectural. Upright growth clears above furniture. Filtered light under gazebo roof suits it well. Overwinter: close fabric walls + mulch.
Filtered lightDog safeRegular water
Ceramic B · Slab Edge (Largest Pot)
Canna 'Tropicanna'
Canna × generalis
Vivid striped foliage — burgundy, red, gold, green — with spectacular orange flowers summer through fall. Matched pair with Tropicanna Black. Full afternoon sun on the slab is perfect.
Full afternoon sunDog safePollinator
Ceramic C · Front Door (Medium Pot)
Hosta 'Sum and Substance'
Hosta × hybrida
Enormous chartreuse-gold leaves glow in morning light. Stunning contrast against the deep burgundy dwarf maple right beside it. Thrives in morning sun + afternoon shade — perfect front door conditions.
Morning sun onlyDog safeKeep moist
Beds · New Additions

Perennial Plantings

Chosen for the PNW climate, habitat value, and your specific light conditions. All dog and baby safe unless noted.

SW Corner Bed
Astilbe 'Fanal'
Astilbe × arendsii
Feathery deep red plumes June–August, exactly when the bleeding hearts go dormant. Fills the gap beautifully under the Japanese maple. Drip irrigation priority for consistent moisture.
Dappled shadeDog safeNeeds moisturePollinator
SW Corner Bed + North Side Yard
Sweet Woodruff
Galium odoratum
Delicate white spring flowers, spreads into a weed-suppressing mat. Smells like fresh hay when brushed. Purpose-built for shaded beds under established trees. Going in both the SW corner and north side yard.
Deep shade okDog safeFragrant
Street-Side Bed
Geranium 'Rozanne'
Geranium × hybridum
Violet-blue flowers from June through hard frost — one of the longest-blooming perennials you can plant. Weaves between iris clumps at ground level. Handles tough conditions and heavy sidewalk dog traffic.
Part shade okDog safePollinator
Container Bed 2
Salvia 'Caradonna'
Salvia nemorosa
Deep violet-purple spikes on near-black stems from June through September. Hummingbirds and butterflies flock to it. Anchor plant for the pollinator bed — upright and dramatic.
Full sunDog safeHummingbirds
Container Bed 2
Agastache 'Blue Fortune'
Agastache foeniculum
Fragrant blue-purple flower spikes July–September attract every pollinator in the neighborhood. Anise-scented foliage deters pests. Pairs perfectly with the Salvia in the pollinator bed.
Full sunDog safeBees + butterfliesFragrant
Container Bed 2
Echinacea 'Magnus'
Echinacea purpurea
Classic coneflower with large flat purple-pink petals. Leave seedheads standing in fall — goldfinches will flock to them all winter. Extremely tough once established, part sun tolerant.
Part sun okDog safeBirds + bees
Front Yard · Structural Plants

Shrubs & Bulbs

Replacing the Asian pear plan with a flowering native shrub that works with the maples rather than competing with them.

N Driveway Bed · Replaces Pear Plan
Fothergilla 'Mt Airy'
Fothergilla major
White bottlebrush flowers in April as the maples leaf out, then fiery orange-red-yellow fall foliage alongside them. Native understory shrub — evolved to live beside trees, not compete. Reaches 4–5ft.
Part shadeDog safeFragrant bloomsFall color
Street-Side Bed · Plant Fall 2026
Allium 'Purple Sensation'
Allium aflatunense
Deep violet globe flowers on tall stems in May–June, right as the bearded iris finishes. Plant bulbs in fall, returns reliably every year. Completes the purple-blue seasonal succession already in this bed.
Full sunDog safeBeesPlant fall

Action Plan

What needs to happen and when.

Immediate
Call Portland BES re: pampas ID · Hire landscaper for pampas + yucca removal
Before April
Order 4 yards soil from Local Soil Supplier · Buy lawn supplies · Confirm gate width
Nursery Run
Portland Nursery SE Division (9000 SE Division) for most plants on this list
Bog Plants
Local Nursery or Specialty Plant Vendor farmers market for Darlingtonia + Drosera
Fall 2026
Plant Allium 'Purple Sensation' bulbs in street bed
Ongoing
Re-route drip irrigation once all plantings finalized

Prices vary by nursery. Planning estimate only.
